name: Leungane y Artzetagan, circular desde Mañaria — Parque Natural de Urkiola
region_intro: Hiking around Mañaria offers access to diverse terrain within the historical territory of Bizkaia. The region is characterized by impressive limestone summits, lush forests, and green meadows. Karstic rock formations are also prevalent, providing varied landscapes for outdoor activities. Mañaria's location, largely surrounded by the Urkiola Natural Park, contributes to its appeal for hikers. Best hiking trails around Mañaria The most popular hiking route is Mugarra — round trip from Mañaria, a 5.1 miles (8.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 33 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends through varied landscapes to the prominent Mugarra summit. Another top favourite among local hikers is Leungane y Artzetagan, circular desde Mañaria — Parque Natural de Urkiola, a moderate 6.4 miles (10.3 km) path. This circular route winds through the Urkiola Natural Park, featuring significant elevation gain. Local hikers also love the Mikeldi Durangaldea Trail, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail leading through the Urkiola Natural Park, often completed in about 4 hours 16 minutes. Hiking around Mañaria is defined by limestone summits, lush forests, and karstic rock formations. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging ascents. The routes in Mañaria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 1200 hikers have used komoot to explore Mañaria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Mañaria?
A: Mañaria offers a diverse selection of over 20 hiking routes. These trails cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy strolls to challenging ascents through the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What makes hiking in Mañaria unique?
A: Hiking in Mañaria is characterized by its stunning natural beauty, largely due to its location within the Urkiola Natural Park. The landscape features impressive limestone summits like Mugarra and Untzillatx, transitioning from lush forests and green meadows to distinctive karstic rock formations. This varied terrain provides a rich and engaging backdrop for outdoor activities.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hikes in Mañaria?
A: The trails around Mañaria offer a range of difficulties. While there is one easy route, the majority are either moderate (7 routes) or difficult (12 routes). Many of the more challenging paths, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1388622153/mugarra-round-trip-from-manaria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mugarra — round trip from Mañaria&lt;/a&gt;, require good fitness, sure-footedness, and sturdy shoes due to significant elevation gains and technical sections.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Mañaria?
A: The hiking routes in Mañaria are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average score of 4.6 stars from over 160 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the challenging ascents to prominent summits, and the natural beauty found within the Urkiola Natural Park.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Mañaria?
A: Yes, Mañaria offers several circular routes. A notable moderate option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1388630529/leungane-y-artzetagan-circular-desde-manaria-parque-natural-de-urkiola" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leungane y Artzetagan, circular desde Mañaria — Parque Natural de Urkiola&lt;/a&gt;, which winds through the Urkiola Natural Park. For more challenging circular options, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5343166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Untzillatx Summit – Atxarte Cliffs loop&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5343163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Untzillatx Summit (941 m) loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on the hikes around Mañaria?
A: Yes, the area around Mañaria features natural attractions like waterfalls. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3009759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uguzpe Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which can be a beautiful point of interest during your hike.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Mañaria?
A: While many routes are challenging, the Urkiola Natural Park, which surrounds Mañaria, offers facilities that can enhance a family outing. The Toki Alai Interpretation Center within the park has a recreational area with tables, fountains, and barbecues, providing a pleasant spot for families to relax before or after shorter walks.

Q: What kind of natural landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see?
A: Mañaria is surrounded by impressive natural landmarks. You can expect to see prominent limestone summits such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anboto (1,331 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alluitz (1034m)&lt;/a&gt;, offering magnificent views from their peaks. The landscape also features unique karstic rock formations and several mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Muniketa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is public transport available to reach Mañaria trailheads?
A: Mañaria is accessible, but specific public transport options directly to all trailheads within the Urkiola Natural Park might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules to Mañaria itself and plan accordingly, as some trailheads may require a short walk or taxi from the town center.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Mañaria and Urkiola Natural Park?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails within natural parks like Urkiola, but they should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Always check specific park regulations or signage at trailheads for any particular restrictions that may apply.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/628313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urkiola Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;, a significant religious building located within the natural park. This site offers both cultural interest and serves as a starting point for several hikes.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Mañaria?
A: The best time for hiking in Mañaria generally depends on the desired experience. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with lush greenery or autumnal colors. Summer can be warm but is suitable for higher altitude trails. Winter hiking is possible but requires appropriate gear and experience, especially on the more challenging, exposed summits.
tips:
This church of Santa María de la Asunción has a nave with an octagonal chancel and a perimeter portico, built in the 16th century. Noteworthy is the complex wooden ribbed vault, which currently maintains its Renaissance character.

It is located in front of the Mañaria car park, where it is normal to see hikers heading towards the peaks of Urkiola.
A good stop along the way.
It is the highest summit of Lagarmendi although its neighbor Mugarra surpasses it in competition because its ascent is not so striking.
Approaching 1000 metres, with a nice mailbox. And you can already see Leungane, the next stop on the route.
An amazing hill, the views are breathtaking, especially those of Mugarra.

waypoints.name: Church of Santa María de la Asunción
